rulings.Case Title: CHATEAU DE LOUIS,LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. GENERAL WINDOW CORPORATION DBA INTERNATIONAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Chateau De Louis, Llc, A California Limited Liability Company, No.Nine, Llc, A California Limited Liability Company, And Real Enterprises, Llc'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or, In The Alternative, That The Matters Involved In This Discovery Be Deemed Admitted, Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2000
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 20, 2013, Line 1, Plaintiffs Chateau De Louis, Llc, A California Limited Liability Company, No.Nine, Llc, A California Limited Liability Company, And Real Enterprises, Llc'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or, In The Alternative, That The Matters Involved In This Discovery Be Deemed Admitted, Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2000. Denied. Terminating sanctions are not appropriate as Plaintiffs have not established an abuse of the discovery. (CCP § 2023.030(d)(1).) Issue sanctions are not appropriate as Plaintiffs have not enumerated or otherwise identified specific designated facts to which the Court may base issue sanctions. (CCP § 2023.030(b).) Plaintiffs have not established a failure to comply with a deposition notice. (CCP § 2025.450.) The Court's inability to ascertain a basis for the requested sanctions is in part based on the failure of Plaintiff to file a Separate Statement as required by CRC 3.1345. This failure is also an independent reason for denying Plaintiffs' motion. Plaintiffs' Motion for Monetary Sanctions is also denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: BRAVO & MARGULIES VS. FRANK M. VILCHES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs Of Deft Jeanette Vilches
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 20, 2013, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT BRAVO & MARGULIES' Motion To Tax Costs Of Defendant Jeanette Vilches. $4264.61 for mediation costs are allowed. An item neither specifically allowable under CCP § 1033.5(a) nor prohibited under § 1033.5(b) may nevertheless be recoverable in the Court's discretion. CCP § 1033.5(c)(4); Ladas v. California State Auto. Ass'n (1993) 19 Cal. App. 4th 761, 773-774; Ripley v. Pappadopoulos (1994) 23 Cal. App. 4th 1616, 1623. Such discretionary costs must be reasonable in amount and "reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation." CCP § 1033.5(c)(2),(3); Davis v. KGO-T.V., Inc. (1998), 17 Cal.4th 436, 444; Ladas v. California State Auto. Ass'n, supra, 19 Cal. App. 4th at 774. The facts presented demonstrate that the mediation costs were reasonably necessary to this litigation. See Gibson v. Bobroff (1996), 49 Cal. App. 4th 1202, 1207-1209. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BEAL et al VS. ROYAL OAK BAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 20, 2013, Line 2. Cross-Complainant ARES PAPAGEORGE'S Motion for Sanction Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants and Cross-Complainants Ares Papageorge and Royal Oak Bar ("Defendants") have filed a motion against Plaintiffs Michael Beal and Ashley Jackson ("Plaintiffs") seeking terminating sanctions and monetary sanctions for misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiffs have not filed an opposition to the motion. Defendants argue that during the course of this litigation, they have been forced to file four motions to compel relevant discovery over Plaintiffs objections. Defendants argue that in connection with each of the four motions, Plaintiffs did not file oppositions and, at the eleventh hour prior to the hearing, agreed to provide the discovery at issue in the motion. Defendants now seek terminating sanctions against Plaintiffs or, in the alternative, monetary sanctions based on Plaintiffs' misuse of the discovery process. Defendants' motion is granted in part and denied in part. The request for terminating sanctions is denied. The discovery statutes "evince an incremental approach to discovery sanctions, starting with monetary sanctions and ending with the ultimate sanctions of termination." Dopes v. Bentley Motors, Inc., 174 Cal. App. 4th 967, 992 (2009). In the usual case, terminating sanctions are not appropriate absent the failure to obey an order compelling discovery. See New Albertsons, Inc. v. Superior Court, 168 Cal. App. 4th 1403, 1424-25 (2009). In this case, there does not appear to have been a prior order compelling discovery, nor does it appear that lesser sanctions have previously been imposed upon Plaintiffs. Accordingly, terminating sanctions are not appropriate at this juncture. Based on the record of apparent misuse of the discovery process, however, Defendants' request for monetary sanctions is granted in the total amount of $10,496.50 to cover Defendants' costs in bringing the four prior motions and the instant motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WINELAND-THOMSON ADVENTURES, INC. DBA THOMSON SAFARIS VS. DOES 1 THROUGH 10, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moton For Order Of Cotnempt And Sanctions Against Failure To Produce Documents Pursuant To Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 20, 2013, Line 7. Plaintiff WINELAND-THOMSON ADVENTURES, INC. dba THOMSON SAFARIS' Motion for Order of Contempt and Sanctions Against Failure to Produce Documents Pursuant to Subpoena Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties are required to appear. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN TERENZIO et al VS. CURRENT TV, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 20, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANTs CURRENT TV, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION, CURRENT MEDIA, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION & AL GORE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Continued to June 20, 2013 to give Defendants the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Defendants must deliver courtesy copies of the reply papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 20, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the demurrer will be overruled. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/RAK)
